pages.title: Stonefish Simulator — Overview
pages.purpose: High-level introduction to the Stonefish marine robotics simulation library: its purpose, key capabilities, architecture overview, and links to all major subsystem pages.

pages.title: Getting Started: Installation and Build System
pages.purpose: Step-by-step guide to installing dependencies, building the library with CMake (BUILD_TESTS, EMBED_RESOURCES options), system-wide installation, and consuming the Stonefish::Stonefish CMake target in external projects.

pages.title: Project Structure and Source Layout
pages.purpose: Map of the repository directories — Library/src, Library/include, 3rdparty, Tests, scripts, docs — explaining what lives where and how the modules relate to each other.

pages.title: Scripts Directory: AUV Docking Simulation
pages.purpose: Details the contents of the scripts/ directory, specifically featuring the ROS2 AUV docking control script. Covers the implemented GNC architecture for Stonefish simulations, including Phase 1 Artificial Potential Field (APF) staging, Phase 2 PLATO kinematic speed profiling with Pure Pursuit, and the Emergency Reflex Layer for mathematical collision avoidance.

page_notes.content:
### 1. Phase 1: Artificial Potential Field (APF) Staging
The APF phase guides the AUV from a random starting position to a safe staging point directly in front of the dock, avoiding the physical dock structure in the process.

The total virtual force acting on the AUV is the sum of three distinct fields: F_total = F_att + F_rep + F_tan.

* Attractive Field (F_att): Pulls the AUV toward the staging point. F_att = K_att * (p_staging - p_auv) / max(d_staging, 0.001)
* Repulsive Field (F_rep): Pushes the AUV away from the dock center, activating only within the influence radius (r_inf). F_rep = K_rep * (1/d - 1/r_inf)^2 * n
* Tangential Field (F_tan): Prevents local minima by creating a clockwise sweeping force. F_tan = K_tan * (1/d - 1/r_inf)^2 * t

### 2. Phase 2: Pure Pursuit Guidance
Once the AUV reaches the staging point, it aligns with the docking axis. Pure Pursuit minimizes the cross-track error to guide the AUV into the dock mouth.
* Path Projection: s_curr = v_MA dot u_path
* Lookahead Calculation: P_goal = P_closest + L * u_path

### 3. Phase 2: PLATO Kinematic Speed Profiler
Ensures a soft latching sequence by dynamically braking the AUV based on velocity and distance.
* Dynamic Braking Radius: R_d(t) = r_d + f_dec * (s_t^2) / (2 * a_max)
* Desired Speed: s_d(t) = s_min + (s_c - s_min) * ( (ln(d_t) - ln(r_d)) / (ln(R_d(t)) - ln(r_d)) )^n_t

### 4. Emergency Reflex Layer (Collision Avoidance)
A mathematical reflex layer defining a keep-out cylinder with a safe entry corridor. If the AUV is physically inside the dock's hazard radius (d_obs < r_hazard) AND has drifted outside the safe entry funnel (XTE > w_corridor), guidance commands are completely overwritten with maximum reverse surge thrust (u_surge = 200.0).

pages.title: Core Simulation Framework
pages.purpose: Overview of the central simulation engine: the SimulationManager lifecycle, application types, robot abstraction, and supporting managers. Links to child pages for each major core component.

pages.title: SimulationManager and Application Lifecycle
pages.purpose: Detailed coverage of SimulationManager (BuildScenario, physics step callbacks, solver types, collision filters), GraphicalSimulationApp vs ConsoleSimulationApp, and the SimulationApp base class.

pages.title: Scenario Definition and XML Parser
pages.purpose: How to define simulation scenarios: the XML scenario file format (environment, materials, bodies, robots, sensors, actuators tags), the ScenarioParser class, include files, and the tinyxml2/tinyexpr integration.

pages.title: Robot Model: Links, Joints, and Kinematics
pages.purpose: How robots (kinematic trees) are constructed from SolidEntity links and joints, the Robot/FeatherstoneRobot/GeneralRobot class hierarchy, Featherstone multi-body algorithm, and joint types (Fixed, Revolute, Prismatic, Cylindrical, Spherical, Spring, Screw).

pages.title: Supporting Core Managers
pages.purpose: NameManager (unique naming), MaterialManager (material properties and friction tables), NED coordinate system, Console logging, FilteredCollisionDispatcher, and TorusShape custom collision shape.

pages.title: Entity System
pages.purpose: Overview of the entity hierarchy — static, dynamic (solid), animated, cable, and forcefield entities — and how they integrate with the physics engine. Links to child pages for each entity category.

pages.title: Static and Dynamic Solid Bodies
pages.purpose: StaticEntity (Plane, Obstacle, Terrain), SolidEntity and its concrete shapes (Box, Sphere, Cylinder, Torus, Polyhedron, Compound, Wing), physical property computation from geometry, hydrodynamics settings, and BodyFluidPosition tracking.

pages.title: Animated Entities and Trajectory System
pages.purpose: AnimatedEntity for scripted motion, trajectory types (BSTrajectory B-spline, CRTrajectory Catmull-Rom, PWLTrajectory piecewise-linear, ManualTrajectory), and the tinyspline integration.

pages.title: Cable Entity
pages.purpose: CableEntity for flexible tether/cable simulation using Bullet SoftBody, CableNodalForces (buoyancy, form drag, skin friction), CableEnds attachment options, and hydrodynamic forces on cables.

pages.title: Forcefields: Ocean, Atmosphere, and Velocity Fields
pages.purpose: ForcefieldEntity base class, Ocean (waves, currents, hydrodynamics settings, Jerlov water types), Atmosphere, and velocity field types (Jet, Pipe, Uniform, VelocityField, Trigger).

pages.title: Hydrodynamics and Physics Engine
pages.purpose: Overview of the physics and hydrodynamics subsystems: Bullet Physics integration, geometry-based hydrodynamic force computation, and the theoretical foundations. Links to child pages.

pages.title: Bullet Physics Integration and Collision
pages.purpose: How Stonefish embeds and configures Bullet Physics (BT_USE_DOUBLE_PRECISION, BT_EULER_DEFAULT_ZYX), the FilteredCollisionDispatcher, collision shapes (convex hull, concave-convex, TorusShape), material friction, and the Stribeck transition model.

pages.title: Geometry-Based Hydrodynamics
pages.purpose: Theory and implementation of geometry-based hydrodynamic force computation: added mass, buoyancy (including partial submersion), quadratic form drag, skin friction, GeometryApproxType, and the HydrodynamicsSettings structure.

pages.title: Actuators
pages.purpose: Overview of the actuator system: joint actuators vs link actuators, the Actuator base class, watchdog timers, and links to specific actuator type pages.

pages.title: Joint Actuators: Motors and Servos
pages.purpose: JointActuator base class, Motor, DCMotor (with electrical model), Servo (position-controlled), and the RotorDynamics models (ZeroOrder, FirstOrder, Yoeger, Bessa, MechanicalPI) defined in ActuatorDynamics.

pages.title: Link Actuators: Thrusters, Propellers, and Marine Devices
pages.purpose: LinkActuator base class, Thruster (with ThrustModel and RotorDynamics), SimpleThruster, Propeller, Rudder (hydrofoil), VariableBuoyancy (VBS), SuctionCup, Push, and Light actuators.

pages.title: Sensors
pages.purpose: Overview of the sensor system: the three sensor groups (joint, link, vision), the Sensor/ScalarSensor/VisionSensor base classes, Sample data structure, noise models, and links to child pages.

pages.title: Scalar Sensors: Joint and Link Sensors
pages.purpose: Joint sensors (RotaryEncoder, RealRotaryEncoder, Torque, ForceTorque, JointSensor), link sensors (IMU, INS, Accelerometer, Gyroscope, Compass, GPS, Pressure, DVL, Odometry, Pose, Current, Multibeam, Profiler, Mux), noise configuration, and the Sample data container.

pages.title: Vision Sensors: Cameras and Acoustic Imaging
pages.purpose: VisionSensor base class, Camera hierarchy (ColorCamera, DepthCamera, SegmentationCamera, ThermalCamera, EventBasedCamera, OpticalFlowCamera), acoustic imaging sensors (FLS, SSS, MSIS, Multibeam2), GPU-accelerated sonar simulation via OpenGL, and output data formats.

pages.title: Communication Devices
pages.purpose: Overview of the underwater communication subsystem: the Comm base class, message lifecycle, and links to acoustic/optical modem and USBL child pages.

pages.title: Acoustic and Optical Modems
pages.purpose: Comm base class (CommDataFrame, RxBuffer/TxBuffer, SDL_mutex thread safety), AcousticModem (propagation delay, speed of sound), OpticalModem (line-of-sight raycasting), Connect/SendMessage/ReadMessage API, and integration with SimulationManager.

pages.title: USBL Acoustic Positioning System
pages.purpose: USBL class hierarchy (USBL → USBLSimple, USBLReal), beacon interrogation and auto-ping, BeaconInfo data structure, USBLSimple idealized model vs USBLReal Gaussian noise model, azimuth/elevation computation, and setNoise/setResolution configuration.

pages.title: Rendering Pipeline
pages.purpose: Overview of the custom OpenGL 4.3 rendering pipeline: its architecture, key subsystems (ocean, atmosphere, sonar visualization, cameras), and links to child pages.

pages.title: OpenGL Pipeline Architecture
pages.purpose: OpenGLPipeline (Render loop, drawing queue), OpenGLContent (mesh/texture management), OpenGLState (state machine), OpenGLView (SDL2 window/context), GLSLShader (GLSL program management), IMGUI (in-simulation HUD), OpenGLPrinter (Freetype text), and RenderSettings/HelperSettings structures.

pages.title: Ocean and Atmosphere Rendering
pages.purpose: OpenGLOcean (FFT-based wave simulation, ocean optics), OpenGLRealOcean vs OpenGLFlatOcean, OpenGLAtmosphere (sky and sun rendering), Jerlov water type data, wavelength-dependent light absorption/scattering, ocean particles, and the GLSL shader files for ocean/atmosphere.

pages.title: Camera and Sonar Visualization
pages.purpose: OpenGLCamera hierarchy (OpenGLRealCamera, OpenGLDepthCamera, OpenGLSegmentationCamera, OpenGLThermalCamera, OpenGLEventBasedCamera, OpenGLOpticalFlowCamera), GPU-accelerated sonar rendering (OpenGLSonar, OpenGLFLS, OpenGLSSS, OpenGLMSIS), OpenGLTrackball, and lighting (OpenGLPointLight, OpenGLSpotLight).
